title: Synthetic-data generation methodology (seed + provenanced params + determinism)
context: The project's primary data mode is synthetic generation calibrated to published aggregate statistics. Without a documented, reproducible methodology, different tasks would use inconsistent generation approaches, making outputs non-reproducible and undermining the proof that no real patient data was used. The methodology must enforce: (1) only published aggregate statistics as inputs, (2) a fixed RNG seed, (3) every parameter provenanced to a cited source, and (4) deterministic output (same seed + params => identical bytes). Determinism also serves as the proof the data is not real. Depends on template-001 for the canonical metadata model's generation fields.
objective: Document the synthetic-data generation methodology that all per-dataset tasks must follow, including the fixed-seed requirement, parameter provenance model, determinism gate, and the process for reviewing clinical plausibility without implying real individuals.
acceptanceCriteria: Specifies that synthetic inputs are only published aggregate statistics (each cited with value) — never individual-level data, even for fitting.
Requires a fixed RNG seed and a parameter file where every parameter carries provenance{citation,url}; output is deterministic.
Defines the determinism/reproducibility check: same seed + params => identical bytes; this is both a quality gate and the proof the data is not real.
Documents how clinical plausibility is reviewed without implying any real individual (domain reviewer role, published reference ranges cited).
Specifies the parameter file format consistent with the generation{} block in the canonical metadata model from template-001.
Includes at least one worked example of a parameter table with citations for a survival-analysis scenario.
Document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md (§Solution — Synthetic generation harness; §Data, licensing & compliance)
SEER/NCI aggregate survival statistics (candidate calibration sources)
output: A synthetic-data generation methodology document specifying the fixed-seed + provenanced-params + determinism requirements, committed to the project repo and used as the specification for tooling-006 and all per-dataset generation tasks.
